[Subagent Context] You are running as a subagent (depth 1/1). Results auto-announce to your requester; do not busy-poll for status.

[Subagent Task]

[CHG-0919] Implement cron model routing / DNS resilience fix

CURRENT STATE (from failed combined run investigation):
- ollama/deepseek-flash returns model_not_found (404) on local Ollama — this model name is stale/incorrect.
- The actual configured cloud-tagged models are recognized locally at http://127.0.0.1:11434: deepseek-v4-flash:cloud, deepseek-v4-pro:cloud, kimi-k2.7-code:cloud, kimi-k2.6:cloud, gemma4:31b-cloud, minimax-m3:cloud.
- During the ollama.com DNS outage, crons using :cloud models failed with "All models failed (N): ollama/: 502 dial tcp: lookup ollama.com: no such host".
- This indicates :cloud-tagged models route to ollama.com when reachable, creating a single point of DNS failure for many governance/operational crons.

WHAT TO DO:
1. Confirm the routing mechanism: inspect /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/openclaw.json models.providers.ollama section, any gateway model routing code/config, and state/model-policy.json to understand how :cloud suffix resolves to ollama.com vs local.
2. Choose and implement the lowest-risk fix that makes crons resilient to an ollama.com outage. Preferred options in order:
   a. Configure the Ollama provider or model routing so that :cloud models fall back to the local endpoint (127.0.0.1:11434) when ollama.com is unreachable, without changing cron payloads.
   b. Add local-only model aliases in openclaw.json for crons to use, and update cron model assignments only where the model policy allows (e.g., use deepseek-v4-flash without :cloud for non-interactive crons).
   c. If no safe config-only fix exists, document the exact root cause, the current routing behavior, and the recommended architecture change; stop for Ken approval before altering model assignments.
3. If you make any config changes to openclaw.json or model-policy.json, back up the original and run `bash /Users/ainchorsoc2a/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/gateway-config-snapshot.sh` to update the baseline.
4. Test the fix by running one or two affected crons manually (`openclaw cron run <id>`) and confirming success while DNS is healthy. Do not simulate outages.

CONSTRAINTS:
- No gateway restart unless Ken approves.
- Do not delete or re-pull Ollama models.
- Do not change cron schedules or payload content unless clearly required and noted.
- Preserve model policy governance (state/model-policy.json is CHG-controlled).

OUTPUT:
Return a concise report: WHAT changed, WHY, test results, any blockers, and what Ken should approve next.

Begin. Execute the assigned task to completion.
